snippets.title: pandas hygiene before pipeline fit
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "user_id": [1, 1, 2],
    "country": [" US", "US", "ca"],
    "spend": ["$10", "$20", "bad"],
})

clean = (
    df.drop_duplicates(subset=["user_id"])
      .assign(country=lambda d: d["country"].str.strip().str.upper())
      .assign(spend=lambda d: pd.to_numeric(d["spend"].str.replace("$", "", regex=False), errors="coerce"))
)
print(clean)
snippets.explanation: Dedupe keys, normalize strings, and coerce numerics so downstream imputers see consistent types and NaN for bad values.

snippets.title: Numeric + categorical ColumnTransformer Pipeline
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

X_train = pd.DataFrame({"age": [25, None, 40], "city": ["A", "B", None]})
y_train = [0, 1, 0]
X_test = pd.DataFrame({"age": [None, 50], "city": ["A", "Z"]})

numeric_pipe = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
])
categorical_pipe = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
    ("encoder", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore")),
])

preprocess = ColumnTransformer([
    ("num", numeric_pipe, ["age"]),
    ("cat", categorical_pipe, ["city"]),
])

clf = Pipeline([
    ("preprocess", preprocess),
    ("model", LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000)),
])
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
print(clf.predict(X_test))
snippets.explanation: One fit on X_train learns medians, category vocabulary, and scaler stats; predict applies the same transforms to X_test including unseen city 'Z' via handle_unknown='ignore'.

snippets.title: FunctionTransformer for reusable pandas clean step
snippets.code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.preprocessing import FunctionTransformer


def strip_upper(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    out = df.copy()
    for col in out.select_dtypes(include="object").columns:
        out[col] = out[col].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()
    return out

ft = FunctionTransformer(strip_upper, validate=False)
X = pd.DataFrame({"region": [" east ", "WEST"]})
print(ft.fit_transform(X))
snippets.explanation: FunctionTransformer wraps stateless pandas logic so it participates in Pipeline fit/transform and serializes with the rest of the steps.

snippets.title: Save and load fitted pipeline with joblib
snippets.code:
import joblib
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
    ("clf", LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000)),
])
# pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)  # after real training

joblib.dump(pipe, "model_pipeline.joblib")
loaded = joblib.load("model_pipeline.joblib")
# loaded.predict(X_production)
snippets.explanation: joblib preserves fitted transformer parameters and model coefficients for identical inference in batch or API jobs.
